Awesome Game Solving

The canonical reference for solved games: every known result, the method that produced it, and the code to verify it.

31 solved games · 14 strongly solved · 9 families · citation-gated · source & contribute on GitHub →

What "solved" means

  • Ultra-weak — the value of the starting position is known (who wins, or draw), but not how.
  • Weak — the value and a strategy to achieve it from the start are known.
  • Strong — the value is known for every reachable position, not just the start (a full tablebase).
GameResultStrength YearFamilySource
Tic-tac-toe3×3 (m,n,k = 3,3,3) Draw strong — abstract folklore
NimSeveral heaps (piles) of counters; players alternately remove one or more counters from a single heap. Classic three-heap demonstration uses heaps of 3, 5, 7. First player wins strong 1901 combinatorial Bouton 1901
Wythoff's gameTwo heaps of counters (unbounded, non-negative integer sizes) First player wins strong 1907 combinatorial Wythoff 1907
Hexapawn3x3 (standard); generalizes to n x m rectangular boards (e.g. 4x4 'octopawn') Second player wins strong 1962 other Gardner 1962
SimComplete graph K6 (6 vertices, 15 edges); each player colors edges in their own color Second player wins strong 1974 other Mead et al. 1974
Qubic4×4×4 cube (64 cells, 76 winning lines) First player wins weak 1980 abstract Patashnik 1980
Mū tōrereEight-pointed star: 8 outer points (kewai) arranged in a ring, each joined to its two neighbours and to a central point (pūtahi); 9 cells total. Four pieces per player, placed on the eight outer points (the centre starts empty). Draw strong 1987 other Ascher 1987
Gomoku (free-style)15×15 First player wins weak 1993 abstract Allis et al. 1993
Nine Men's Morris24-point morris board (three concentric squares joined by midpoint connectors), 9 stones per player Draw strong 1993 abstract Gasser 1993
Connect Four7x6 (7 columns wide, 6 rows high), gravity drop First player wins strong 1995 abstract Tromp 1995
Pentominoes (two-player)8×8 board, 12 distinct (free) pentomino pieces First player wins weak 1996 combinatorial Orman 1996
KalahTwo rows of 6 holes plus two stores (kalahah); standard commercial game starts with 4 seeds per hole — Kalah(6,4). Notation Kalah(m,n) = m holes per side, n initial seeds per hole. First player wins weak 2000 mancala Irving et al. 2000
Renju (連珠)15x15 grid (stones on intersections); Black plays first and must open on the center point h8 First player wins weak 2001 abstract Wágner et al. 2001
Awari (Oware)2×6 pits, 4 seeds per pit (48 seeds total) Draw strong 2002 mancala Romein et al. 2002
Go (5×5)5x5 square Go board (25 intersections) First player wins weak 2002 go van der Werf 2002
Hex (7×7)7×7 rhombus of hexagonal cells (49 cells); each player owns two opposite sides, first player connects their pair; no-swap (no pie rule) ruleset First player wins weak 2003 connection Yang et al. 2003
Lasker MorrisNine Men's Morris board (24 points / intersections, no diagonals); 10 men per player (both Stahlhacke's headline 136-billion-position database and Gévay & Danner solve the ten-men variant, per Lasker's "nine or better ten") Draw strong 2003 abstract Stahlhacke 2003
Checkers (English draughts)8x8 board, pieces on dark squares only (32 playable squares) Draw weak 2007 abstract Schaeffer et al. 2007
Fanorona5x9 (Fanoron-Tsivy), 45 intersections of lines; 22 pieces per side at start Draw weak 2007 other Schadd et al. 2007
Tigers and Goats5x5 grid of 25 points connected by horizontal, vertical and diagonal lines (Bagh-Chal board). Asymmetric: 4 tigers (start on the 4 corner points) face up to 20 goats. Goat moves first. Draw weak 2007 other Jin et al. 2007
Dōbutsu shōgi3×4 (3 files × 4 ranks) Second player wins strong 2009 shogi-variant Tanaka 2009
Gardner minichess5x5 (smallest board holding all standard chess piece types on the back rank) Draw weak 2013 chess-variant Mhalla et al. 2013
Hex (9×9, all openings)9×9 rhombus of hexagonal cells (81 cells); two opposite sides per player, first player connects their pair; no-swap (no pie rule) ruleset First player wins weak 2013 connection Pawlewicz et al. 2013
Pentago6×6 with four rotatable 3×3 quadrants First player wins strong 2014 abstract Irving 2014
Amazons (small boards)5x6 rectangular grid (headline); also solved: 4x4, 5x5, 4x5, 5x4, 4x6, 4x7, 6x4 First player wins weak 2015 combinatorial Song et al. 2015
Heads-up Limit Texas Hold'em (HULHE)Two-player limit Texas hold'em; 52-card deck, four betting rounds (preflop/flop/turn/river), fixed bet sizes, capped raises per round, alternating dealer/button First player wins (ε-Nash) weak (approx.) 2015 imperfect-info Bowling et al. 2015
MorabarabaNine Men's Morris board with the four diagonal lines added (24 points / intersections, all corners of the three nested squares connected by diagonals); 12 men per player First player wins strong 2015 abstract Gévay 2015
DomineeringSquare and rectangular grids; headline result is 11x11. Solved instances range from 1x1 up to 11x11 squares plus many m x n rectangles. First player wins weak 2016 combinatorial Uiterwijk 2016
Losing chessStandard 8x8 chessboard, orthodox starting position; captures compulsory, non-royal king, no castling, promotion to king allowed First player wins weak 2016 chess-variant Watkins 2016
Othello8x8 (64 squares; standard cross start with 4 discs placed) Draw weak 2023 abstract Takizawa 2023
Quarto!4×4 board, 16 distinct pieces (each a unique combination of 4 binary attributes; standard line-win ruleset — rows, columns, two diagonals) Draw strong 2023 other Goossens 2023
No games match.
Generated from structured data — the dataset is the product. The README table and this page render from data/games/*.yaml. Unsolved games live on the frontier, never as catalog entries. Released under CC0.
